Block.description: block defines a section on the template and identifies it with a name. This is used by template inheritance. Base templates can specify blocks and child templates can override them with new content.

Complete Variable.description: A variable looks up a value from the template context.

Extends.description: extends is used to specify template inheritance. The specified template is used as a base template.

Include.description: Include pulls in other templates in place.It"s useful when you need to share smaller chunks across several templates that already inherit other templates.

Filter.description: A filter block allows you to call a filter with the contents of the block. Instead passing a value with the | syntax, the render contents from the block will be passed.

For.description: for iterates over arrays and dictionaries.

asyncEach.description: asyncEach is an asynchronous version of for. You only need this if you are using a custom template loader that is asynchronous; otherwise you will never need it. Async filters and extensions also need this, but internally loops are automatically converted into asyncEach if any async filters and extensions are used within the loop.

asyncAll.description: asyncAll is similar to asyncEach, except it renders all the items in parallel, preserving the order of the items. This is only helpful if you are using asynchronous filters, extensions, or loaders. Otherwise you should never use this.

If.description: if tests a condition and lets you selectively display content. It behaves exactly as javascript"s if behaves.

If Else.description: It creates the if else block.

If Elif.description: Alternate condition with the if block

Elif.description: Alternate condition in the if block

Else.description: Alternate branch in an if block (Nunjucks else takes no condition; use elif for conditions).

Set.description: set lets you create/modify a variable.

Macro.description: macro allows you to define reusable chunks of content. It is similar to a function in a programming language.

Import.description: import loads a different template and allows you to access its exported values. Macros and top-level assignments (done with set) are exported from templates, allowing you to access them in a different template.

From Import.description: It imports specific values from a template into the current namespace.

Raw.description: If you want to output any of the special nunjucks tags like {{, you can use raw and anything inside of it will be output as plain text.

Call.description: A call block enables you to call a macro with all the text inside the tag. This is helpful if you want to pass a lot of content into a macro. The content is available inside the macro as caller().

Alt Variable.description: A variable looks up a value from the template context. If you wanted to simply display a variable, you would do: {{ username }}

Super.description: You can render the contents of the parent block inside a child block by calling super.

Pipe.description: You can pass filters from the variable directly e.g. {{ foo | escape }}

Alpine x-data.description: AlpineJS: Declare an Alpine component scope and its reactive data object.

Alpine x-data Block.description: AlpineJS: A wrapper div with x-data reactive state.

Alpine x-show.description: AlpineJS: Toggle element visibility based on a reactive expression (display: none).

Alpine x-model.description: AlpineJS: Two-way bind an input element to reactive data.

Alpine x-model.number.description: AlpineJS: Two-way bind with the .number modifier to coerce numeric input.

Alpine x-text.description: AlpineJS: Set the element's text content from a reactive expression.

Alpine x-html.description: AlpineJS: Set the element's inner HTML from a reactive expression.

Alpine x-bind / :.description: AlpineJS x-bind shorthand: reactively set an HTML attribute (:class, :value, :disabled ...).

Alpine :class conditional.description: AlpineJS: Conditionally apply a class based on a reactive expression.

Alpine @click.description: AlpineJS x-on shorthand: listen for click events.

Alpine @keydown.description: AlpineJS: Listen for keyboard events with a key modifier.

Alpine x-for.description: AlpineJS: Loop over an array, rendering the template content for each item.

Alpine x-if.description: AlpineJS: Conditionally insert/remove elements from the DOM (use template tag).

Alpine x-ref.description: AlpineJS: Register a reference to the element, accessible via $refs.

Alpine x-transition.description: AlpineJS: Apply smooth enter/leave transitions on x-show elements.

Alpine x-cloak.description: AlpineJS: Hide the element until Alpine has initialized (pair with [x-cloak] { display: none; }).

Alpine x-init.description: AlpineJS: Run an expression when an Alpine component is initialized.

Alpine x-effect.description: AlpineJS: Re-run an expression whenever any of its reactive dependencies change.

Alpine $store.description: AlpineJS magic: Access a registered Alpine global store.

Alpine $dispatch.description: AlpineJS magic: Dispatch a custom browser event.

Alpine $refs.description: AlpineJS magic: Access an element registered with x-ref.

Alpine $nextTick.description: AlpineJS magic: Run code after the DOM has reacted to data changes.

body: <div x-show="open" @click.outside="open = false" x-transition class="${3:absolute right-0 mt-2 w-48 bg-white rounded shadow-lg}">
Alpine Dropdown Pattern.description: AlpineJS + Tailwind: A complete dropdown menu with click-outside dismiss and transition.

body: <button @click="open = true" class="${1:px-4 py-2 bg-blue-500 text-white rounded}">${2:Open}</button>
<div x-show="open" x-cloak class="fixed inset-0 z-50 flex items-center justify-center bg-black/50" @keydown.escape.window="open = false">
<div class="${3:bg-white rounded-lg p-6 max-w-md w-full mx-4}" @click.outside="open = false">
<button @click="open = false" class="${4:mt-4 px-4 py-2 bg-gray-200 rounded}">Close</button>
Alpine Modal Pattern.description: AlpineJS + Tailwind: A complete modal dialog with ESC close and backdrop.

Alpine Tabs Pattern.description: AlpineJS + Tailwind: A complete tab navigation component with reactive content switching.

body: class="relative inline-flex h-6 w-11 items-center rounded-full transition-colors">
class="inline-block h-4 w-4 transform rounded-full bg-white transition-transform">
Alpine Toggle Button.description: AlpineJS + Tailwind: An accessible toggle switch component.
